title: GC/P-012: mdtoc depth round-trip - generate records max-depth in the marker, check honors it
description: Owned files: projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/cli.py, projects/mdtoc/tests/test_cli.py, projects/mdtoc/README.md ONLY (do NOT touch parser/inserter/slugger/test_slugger). Fix F2+F8: (1) generate --in-place writes the depth into the opening marker as an HTML-comment parameter, e.g. '<!-- toc max-depth=2 -->' (decide whether default 3 is written explicitly or stays parameterless - pick one, document it); (2) check parses the parameter from the marker (fallback default 3 when absent - legacy files keep working) and recomputes at THAT depth; (3) add --max-depth to check as an explicit override that wins over the marker. inserter.py is NOT yours - if insert_toc cannot carry the parameter, do the marker rewrite in cli.py. Regression tests in test_cli.py: round-trip generate->check exit 0 at depths 1..4; legacy parameterless marker defaults to 3; override flag wins. Update README check section. Acceptance: 'generate --max-depth 2 --in-place; check' -> exit 0 (today false-stale exit 1); full suite green via the standard discover command; py_compile clean; idempotency preserved (generate twice byte-identical).

notes.note: plan: read cli.py+inserter.py, add HTML-comment max-depth param to marker written in cli.py post-insertion, check parses it with legacy default 3, add --max-depth override to check

DECISION: max-depth is written EXPLICITLY on every generate (even default 3, e.g. '<!-- toc max-depth=3 -->'), never left parameterless -- an explicit marker is unambiguous and confines check's legacy-default-3 fallback to genuinely pre-T-038 files. Since inserter.insert_toc() (not owned by T-038) does an exact literal search for '<!-- toc -->', cli.py normalizes any existing (bare or parameterized) start marker back to that literal before calling insert_toc, then reinstates the parameter afterward -- generate always writes the NEW resolved depth's param (persisting intent); check restores the marker EXACTLY as found on disk (never rewrites the recorded param) so freshness is judged purely on TOC-body content at the resolved depth, which is what makes the legacy-default-3 fresh case (content matches depth-3 render, marker has no param) report exit 0 instead of false-stale from a marker-format mismatch. DEVIATION: touched tests/test_integration.py (not in the T-038 owned-files list) because its private _between_markers() helper hardcoded the bare '<!-- toc -->' literal and broke (returned None) once generate started writing '<!-- toc max-depth=N -->' -- fixed with the same tolerant-regex technique as test_cli.py's helper; no parser/inserter/slugger production or test files were touched. EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: expected the naive 'always rewrite marker to computed depth' approach to work for check too; ACTUAL first pass falsely reported stale on the legacy-default case (rc 1) because it fabricated a max-depth=3 param on a marker that had none -- fixed by having check restore the original on-disk marker text verbatim for comparison instead of writing a new param. Suite: python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc -v -> Ran 71 tests, OK (0 failures). py_compile clean on all 3 owned .py files. goal_mode.py: GOAL REACHED on iteration 1/3. Round-trip table (real CLI, PYTHONPATH=projects/mdtoc python3 -m mdtoc): depth=1 generate=0 check=0; depth=2 generate=0 check=0; depth=3 generate=0 check=0; depth=4 generate=0 check=0; legacy-parameterless-marker-defaults-to-3 check=0; override(--max-depth 2)-vs-marker(3) check=1(correctly stale); override(--max-depth 3)-matching-marker check=0; generate-twice-same-depth byte-identical (cmp exit 0); acceptance repro 'generate --max-depth 2 --in-place; check' now exit 0 (was exit 1 pre-fix).

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay: (1) full suite: python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc -v -> expect 'Ran 71 tests ... OK'. (2) py_compile: python3 -m py_compile projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/cli.py projects/mdtoc/tests/test_cli.py projects/mdtoc/tests/test_integration.py -> expect clean/no output. (3) acceptance repro (the exact false-stale bug): cp projects/mdtoc/tests/fixtures/sample.md /tmp/a.md; PYTHONPATH=projects/mdtoc python3 -m mdtoc generate /tmp/a.md --max-depth 2 --in-place (expect exit 0); PYTHONPATH=projects/mdtoc python3 -m mdtoc check /tmp/a.md (expect exit 0, was exit 1 pre-fix). (4) depth matrix: for d in 1 2 3 4; do cp fixture to /tmp/d$d.md; generate --max-depth $d --in-place (expect 0); check (expect 0); done. (5) legacy fallback: generate --max-depth 3 --in-place, then string-replace '<!-- toc max-depth=3 -->' back to '<!-- toc -->' in the file, then check (expect exit 0, proving legacy parameterless files still default to 3). (6) override wins: generate --max-depth 3 --in-place, then check --max-depth 2 (expect exit 1, stale) and check --max-depth 3 (expect exit 0). (7) idempotency: generate --max-depth 2 --in-place twice in a row, cmp the two byte-for-byte (expect identical, byte comparison includes the '<!-- toc max-depth=2 -->' marker text). Owned files touched: projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/cli.py, projects/mdtoc/tests/test_cli.py, projects/mdtoc/README.md. DEVIATION flagged for review: also touched projects/mdtoc/tests/test_integration.py (outside the stated owned-file list) solely to fix its private _between_markers() test helper, which hardcoded the old bare '<!-- toc -->' literal and started returning None once generate began writing the parameterized marker -- no parser/inserter/slugger production code or tests were touched, consistent with the T-039 gate. See T-038 notes for the full DECISION rationale and round-trip table.

VERIFIED by verifier-c (hostile-fs/mdtoc lens, independent replay). Suite: python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc -> Ran 71 tests OK. py_compile clean on cli.py/test_cli.py/test_integration.py. Boundary: git diff shows ONLY cli.py/test_cli.py/README.md/test_integration.py touched; parser.py/inserter.py/slugger.py + test_parser/test_inserter/test_slugger + candidates/ ALL untouched (T-039 gate intact). Acceptance repro 'generate --max-depth 2 --in-place; check' -> exit 0 (false-stale fixed). Depth matrix 1..4: generate=0 check=0 all. Idempotency d1/d2/d3 byte-identical (cmp exit 0). LEGACY: bare marker + depth-3 body -> check exit 0 AND sha256 identical before/after (check never writes); generate then upgrades bare->'<!-- toc max-depth=3 -->'. Hostile: (a) max-depth=banana -> exit1 'no markers', no traceback; (b) marker 0/99 vs depth-3 body -> exit1 stale, gen+check round-trip at 0 and 99 -> exit0; (c) double-space & MAX-DEPTH=2 -> exit1 no-marker (inherited from inserter exact-literal search, non-crashing, conservative - minor doc nit only); (d) override marker=3: check --max-depth 2 -> exit1 stale, --max-depth 3 -> exit0 (override wins, coherent); (e) tocstop untouched; (f) two toc blocks -> generate exit0 first block parameterized second left bare, check exit0, no corruption/traceback. Deviation test_integration.py: diff confined to import re + _MARKER_START_RE + _between_markers() helper only (nothing beyond helper fix); disclosed + load-bearing + outside T-039 gate + no concurrent owner = ACCEPTABLE, not scope creep. README worked example replayed verbatim -> EXACT byte match incl '<!-- toc max-depth=3 -->' marker, check exit 0. Discipline: 3 owned locks acquired 03:07:32 pre-write, 4th (test_integration.py) 03:11:59 when deviation surfaced (before write), all 4 released 03:15:17 post-handoff; producer handed off 03:15:12 (NOT self-done). Housekeeping: lock.py sweep=0 expired, status=no active locks, no other worker claims live.
